name: El Terril and Peñón de Algámitas Loop
name_original: De Pruna a Villanueva de San Juan – circular por la Sierra del Tablón y Algámitas
description: A difficult 29-mile road cycling route from Pruna to Villanueva de San Juan, featuring the Sierra del Tablón and El Peñón de Algámitas.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Pruna are set within the Sierra Sur of Seville, offering diverse landscapes for cyclists. The region features mountainous terrain, including the foothills of the Sierra del Tablón and El Terril Peak, the highest point in Seville province. Cyclists can navigate routes through natural passages like the Estrechón Canyon, carved by the Guadalporcún River, and along the historic Vía Verde de la Sierra, which follows an old railway line through tunnels and over viaducts. This area provides a mix of challenging climbs and scenic paths, making it suitable for varied road bike trails Pruna. Best road cycling routes around Pruna The most popular road cycling route is Setenil de las Bodegas from Olvera – circular via Alcalá del Valle, a 27.2 miles (43.7 km) trail that takes 2 hours 19 minutes to complete. This moderate route connects several towns, offering a varied cycling experience. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is El Terril and Peñón de Algámitas Loop, a difficult 29.0 miles (46.6 km) path. This route features significant elevation gain, traversing the mountainous landscape around El Terril Peak. Local road cyclists also love the Vía Verde de la Sierra – Vía Verde de la Sierra loop from Olvera, a 23.9 miles (38.5 km) trail leading through the historic greenway, often completed in about 2 hours 30 minutes. Road cycling around Pruna is defined by mountainous terrain, river canyons, and historic greenways. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to challenging climbs. The routes in Pruna are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.9 stars from over 7 reviews. More than 580 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Pruna's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Pruna?
A: There are over 40 road cycling routes around Pruna, offering a diverse range of experiences. These include 11 moderate routes and 31 more challenging options, ensuring there's something for various skill levels.

Q: What is the general difficulty level of road cycling routes in Pruna?
A: Road cycling routes in Pruna range from moderate to difficult. While there are a few easier options, the region is characterized by mountainous terrain, offering significant elevation gains. For example, the difficult route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995600004/el-terril-and-penon-de-algamitas-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Terril and Peñón de Algámitas Loop&lt;/a&gt; features substantial climbs.

Q: Are there any challenging road cycling climbs in the Pruna area?
A: Yes, Pruna is situated at the foothills of the Sierra del Tablón, home to El Terril Peak, the highest point in Seville province. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995600004/el-terril-and-penon-de-algamitas-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Terril and Peñón de Algámitas Loop&lt;/a&gt; offer significant elevation gain, traversing this mountainous landscape and providing challenging climbs for experienced cyclists.

Q: Can I cycle along the Vía Verde de la Sierra?
A: Absolutely. The Vía Verde de la Sierra is a major highlight for cyclists in the region. This 36-kilometer green route follows an old railway line, taking riders through tunnels and over viaducts. You can explore it on routes such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38285425" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde de la Sierra – Vía Verde de la Sierra loop from Olvera&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a scenic and unique cycling experience.

Q: What natural attractions can I see while road cycling near Pruna?
A: The region boasts several natural attractions. You can encounter the dramatic Estrechón Canyon, carved by the Guadalporcún River, and the impressive Peñón de Zaframagón. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3795122" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zaframagón Viaduct&lt;/a&gt; is a notable landmark along the Vía Verde. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4048502" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico del Terril&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5391389" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñón de Algámitas&lt;/a&gt; offer stunning views.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or cultural sights along the cycling routes?
A: Yes, the area is rich in history. The Vía Verde de la Sierra itself is a historic route. Nearby, you can visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7520502" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Olvera&lt;/a&gt; and the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7520524" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Archpriest Church of Our Lady of the Incarnation and Saint Joseph&lt;/a&gt; in Olvera. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5459280" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torre Alháquime and Arco de la Villa&lt;/a&gt; also offer glimpses into the region's past.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy the most about road cycling in Pruna?
A: The road cycling routes around Pruna are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 3.9 stars. Cyclists often praise the diverse terrain, from challenging mountainous sections to scenic greenways, and the opportunity to explore charming villages and natural landmarks.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes around Pruna?
A: Many of the road cycling routes in the Pruna area are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e979435382/setenil-de-las-bodegas-from-olvera-circular-via-alcala-del-valle" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Setenil de las Bodegas from Olvera – circular via Alcalá del Valle&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38285425" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde de la Sierra – Vía Verde de la Sierra loop from Olvera&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Pruna?
A: The region generally offers pleasant conditions for road cycling for much of the year. Spring and autumn typically provide mild temperatures, making them ideal for exploring the diverse landscapes without the intense heat of summer.

Q: Are there any viewpoints offering panoramic vistas along the routes?
A: Yes, the mountainous terrain around Pruna provides several opportunities for stunning views. The Puerto de la Ventana Viewpoint offers unique vistas of the Sierra Sur region. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7215298" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Andalusia Square in Olvera&lt;/a&gt; is also noted as a viewpoint.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by a waterfall?
A: While not directly on the listed road cycling routes, the beautiful La Cascada waterfall is located just one kilometer from Pruna. It's a striking 30-meter-high natural landmark that can be visited as a short detour or part of a combined exploration of the area.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Pruna?
A: You can expect a varied terrain. Routes often combine smooth asphalt roads with sections that traverse mountainous landscapes, including significant climbs and descents. The Vía Verde de la Sierra offers a flatter, more consistent surface, while other routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40019925" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trejo River Walk – Setenil de las Bodegas loop from Olvera&lt;/a&gt; can include more challenging ascents.
tips: Pretty town with white houses, with the Castillo de Hierro located on a 663-meter rocky hill that once protected the municipality.

A small town located in the Sierra del Tablón. It is at the foot of El Peñón de Algámitas, the true symbol of this municipality. El Peñón is the highest point in the province of Seville (with 1121 masl).
Ascent from Villanueva de San Juan along the road in very good condition, although with some patched sections. There is no source, so you have to get your water supply in the town.

faqs.question: How long does it take to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: This challenging road cycling route is approximately 46.6 kilometers long and typically takes around 2 hours and 33 minutes to complete. This duration is an estimate and can vary based on your fitness level and stops along the way.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route is categorized as a hard road ride, featuring significant elevation changes. You'll encounter a total elevation gain of 930 meters, with the highest point reaching 800 meters and the lowest at 330 meters. Be prepared for some segments that may be unpaved, adding to the difficulty for road cyclists. The ascent from Villanueva de San Juan to La Calera Pass is generally on a road in good condition, though it may have some patched sections.

faqs.question: What are the main landmarks or scenic points along the way?
faqs.answer: The route traverses the stunning Sierra del Tablón, offering views of dense vegetation like holm oaks and wild olive trees, and extensive olive groves. You'll pass through the pretty town of Pruna, known for its white houses and castle. The trail also takes you near Algámitas, nestled at the foot of El Peñón de Algámitas, which is the highest point in Seville province. You'll also encounter El Terril (1129m), the highest peak in Seville.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this route is described as a 'difficult' and 'hard road ride' that demands a high level of fitness. It features significant elevation changes and some potentially unpaved segments, making it unsuitable for beginners or those seeking a leisurely ride.

faqs.question: Where can I park and access the trailhead?
faqs.answer: The route is circular and starts from Pruna. You can typically find parking within the town to begin your ride. Specific trailhead parking details are not provided, but starting from the town center is a common approach for circular routes.

faqs.question: Are there any water sources available on the trail?
faqs.answer: It's important to note that there are no water sources available at La Calera Pass. You should carry sufficient water with you from the towns of Pruna or Algámitas before embarking on the ride.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: While specific recommendations for this route aren't provided, generally, spring and autumn offer the most pleasant cycling conditions in Andalusia, avoiding the extreme heat of summer and potential chill of winter. Always check local weather forecasts before you go.

faqs.question: Is the route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: This is a road cycling route, and while dogs are generally allowed in public areas, it's not typically considered a 'dog-friendly' trail in the same way a hiking trail might be. Cycling with a dog, especially on a challenging road route with traffic, requires careful consideration for their safety and the safety of others.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: There are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this public road cycling route through the Sierra del Tablón.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route overlap with other official trails. You will briefly pass through parts of the [CIMA SV04] Peñón de Algámitas, El Saucejo-Villanueva de San Juan, and Subida a El Terril.

faqs.question: What gear should I bring for this challenging ride?
faqs.answer: Given the 'difficult' rating and significant elevation, you should bring a well-maintained racebike, appropriate cycling attire, a helmet, repair kit, and plenty of water and snacks. Consider bringing navigation tools as well, as some segments might be less familiar.
